title: Mailbox Size and Quota Trending
app: `Splunk_TA_MS_O365`, Exchange Online / M365 reporting, mailbox stats API
spl:
index=o365 sourcetype="exchange:mailbox_stats"
| stats latest(mailbox_size_mb) as size_mb, latest(quota_mb) as quota_mb by user, mailbox_type
| eval used_pct=round((size_mb/quota_mb)*100, 1)
| where used_pct >= 80
| sort -used_pct
description: Mailbox growth and quota usage help plan storage and avoid user lockouts. Trending supports capacity planning and proactive quota increases.
value: Mailbox growth and quota usage help plan storage and avoid user lockouts. Trending supports capacity planning and proactive quota increases.
implementation: Ingest mailbox size and quota from Graph API or Exchange reporting. Alert when usage exceeds 80% of quota. Report on top consumers and growth rate by department.
visualization: Table (mailboxes near quota), Bar chart (size by user), Line chart (growth trend).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_MS_O365`, Exchange Online / M365 reporting, mailbox stats API.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: Mailbox size and quota metrics.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Ingest mailbox size and quota from Graph API or Exchange reporting. Alert when usage exceeds 80% of quota. Report on top consumers and growth rate by department.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=o365 sourcetype="exchange:mailbox_stats"
| stats latest(mailbox_size_mb) as size_mb, latest(quota_mb) as quota_mb by user, mailbox_type
| eval used_pct=round((size_mb/quota_mb)*100, 1)
| where used_pct >= 80
| sort -used_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**Mailbox Size and Quota Trending** ‚Äî Mailbox growth and quota usage help plan storage and avoid user lockouts. Trending supports capacity planning and proactive quota increases.

Documented **Data sources**: Mailbox size and quota metrics. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_MS_O365`, Exchange Online / M365 reporting, mailbox stats API. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: o365; **sourcetype**: exchange:mailbox_stats. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=o365, sourcetype="exchange:mailbox_stats". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by user, mailbox_type** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **used_pct** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where used_pct >= 80` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare with CUCM RTMT, Cisco collaboration admin, or the vendor‚Äôs own status and CDR/CMR views for the same cluster and time window.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (mailboxes near quota), Bar chart (size by user), Line chart (growth trend).
grandmaExplanation: We use this to stay ahead of email and collaboration problems, so the team is not the last to know when something drifts or breaks.
knownFalsePositives: Archive projects, large mailbox migrations, and journal mailboxes that sit near quota by design.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable; primary SPL uses raw or vendor-normalized event fields.
